title: Programmable keys and security devices
abstract: A security system is provided. The security system includes one or more security devices each configured to protect an item of merchandise from theft and a master key comprising a code stored in memory. The security system also includes one or more user keys configured to communicate with the master key for receiving and storing the code. The one or more security devices are configured to communicate with the master key and/or the one or more user keys for receiving and storing the code, and each user key is configured to communicate with the one or more security devices for arming or disarming thereof upon a matching of the code stored by the user key with the code stored by the security device.
claims:
Claims That which is claimed is: 1. A security system for protecting items of merchandise from theft comprising: one or more security devices each configured to protect an item of merchandise from theft; a master key comprising a code stored in a memory thereof; and one or more user keys configured to communicate with the master key for receiving and storing the code, wherein the one or more security devices are configured to communicate with the master key and/or the one or more user keys for receiving and storing the code, and wherein each user key is configured to communicate with the one or more security devices for operating the one or more security devices upon a matching of the code stored by the user key with the code stored by the security device. 2. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the code is unique to the master key. 3. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the one or more security devices are configured to communicate with the master key for receiving and storing the code. 4. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the one or more security devices are configured to communicate with the one or more user keys for receiving and storing the code. 5. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the code is randomly generated prior to being stored by the master key. 6. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the code is unknown to users of the one or more user keys and the master key. 7. The security system of Claim 1, wherein each user key comprises an opening configured to receive a portion of the master key for communicating therewith. 8. The security system of Claim 1, further comprising a plurality of user keys having the same code as the master key. 9. The security system of Claim 1, further comprising a plurality of security devices having the same code as the master key and the one or more user keys. 10. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the master key is not configured to generate the code. 11. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the code is permanently stored in the memory of the master key. 12. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the master key and the one or more user keys each comprises one or more electrical contacts configured to align and mate with one another for facilitating communication between the master key and the one or more user keys. 13. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the one or more user keys are configured to wirelessly communicate with the one or more security devices. 14. The security system of Claim 1, wherein each user key is configured to communicate with the one or more security devices for arming or disarming the one or more security devices upon a matching of the code stored by the user key with the code stored by the security device. 15. The security system of Claim 1, wherein each of the one or more user keys is configured to remove the code from the security device when engaged with the master key and in communication with the security device. 16. The security system of Claim 1, wherein each of the one or more user keys is configured to be deactivated after a predetermined time period, and wherein the master key is configured to reactivate the one or more user keys that have been deactivated. 17. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the master key is configured to reprogram the one or more user keys. 18. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the master key is configured electrically 

 engage each of the one or more user keys for communication therewith. 19. A method for protecting items of merchandise from theft comprising: providing a master key comprising a code stored in memory; providing one or more security devices each configured to protect an item of merchandise from theft; providing one or more user keys configured to communicate with the master key for receiving and storing the code; causing the master key and/or the one or more user keys to communicate with the one or more security devices for receiving and storing the code; and causing the one or more user keys to communicate with the one or more security devices for operating thereof upon a matching of the code stored by the user key with the code stored by the one or more security devices. 20. The method of Claim 19, wherein causing the master key and/or the one or more user keys to communicate with the one or more security devices comprises causing the one or more user keys to communicate with the one or more security devices for receiving and storing the code. 21. The method of Claim 19, further comprising inserting a portion of the master key within an opening defined in the one or more user keys for communicating therewith. 22. The method of Claim 21, wherein inserting comprises inserting the portion of the master key within an opening defined in each of the one or more user keys. 23. The method of Claim 19, wherein causing the one or more user keys to communicate with the one or more security devices comprises causing the one or more user keys to communicate with the one or more security devices for arming or disarming thereof upon a matching of the code stored by the user key with the code stored by the one or more security devices. 24. The method of Claim 19, wherein causing the one or more user keys to 

 communicate with the one or more security devices comprises causing the one or more user keys to wirelessly communicate with the one or more security devices. 25. A security system for protecting items of merchandise from theft comprising: one or more security devices each configured to protect an item of merchandise from theft; a master key comprising a code stored in a memory thereof; and one or more user keys configured to communicate with the master key for receiving and storing the code, wherein the one or more security devices are configured to communicate with the one or more user keys for receiving and storing the code, and wherein each user key is configured to communicate with the one or more security devices for operating the one or more security devices upon a matching of the code stored by the user key with the code stored by the security device. 26. A security system for protecting items of merchandise from theft comprising: one or more security devices each configured to protect an item of merchandise from theft, the one or more security devices comprising a code stored in a memory thereof; a master key comprising a code stored in a memory thereof; and at least one user key configured to electrically engage with the master key for communicating with the master key to receive and store the code, wherein the at least one user key is configured to communicate with the one or more security devices for operating the one or more security devices upon a matching of the code stored by the at least one user key with the code stored by the security device. 27. The security system of Claim 26 wherein the one or more security devices are configured to communicate with the master key and/or the one or more user keys for receiving and storing the code. 28. The security system of Claim 26, wherein the at least one user key is configured to wirelessly communicate with the one or more security devices. 29. A method for protecting items of merchandise from theft comprising: providing a master key comprising a code stored in memory; providing one or more security devices each configured to protect an item of merchandise from theft; providing at least one user key configured to communicate with the master key for receiving and storing the code; electrically engaging the master key with the at least one user key for communicating with the one or more security devices to receive and store the code; and causing the at least one user key to communicate with the one or more security devices for operating thereof upon a matching of the code stored by the at least one user key with the code stored by the one or more security devices. 30. The method of Claim 29, wherein causing the at least one user key to communicate with the one or more security devices comprises causing the at least one user key to wirelessly communicate with the one or more security devices. 31. The method of Claim 29, wherein electrically engaging the master key with the at least one user key comprises inserting a portion of the master key within an opening defined in the at least one user key.
